mechanism_explained: Tumors stall at the ~1-2 mm diffusion limit until the HIF-1a-VEGF loop flips the angiogenic switch and recruits a blood supply.
Anti-VEGF drugs act by transiently normalizing tumor vasculature, not starving it; reframes timing, resistance, immunotherapy combos.
Invasion is acquired: EMT loss of adhesion, MMP matrix degradation, motility; basement-membrane breach separates in situ from lethal.
Metastasis is an inefficient selective cascade; seed-and-soil organ compatibility and dormancy decide colonization and recurrence.
A tumor is an organ-like ecosystem (CAFs, immune cells, vasculature, ECM, microbiome) that shapes drug delivery, immune access, prognosis.
A wound that never heals: chronic inflammation and matrix remodeling causally drive carcinogenesis and resistance.
Tumors show some hierarchy with therapy-resistant regenerative cells, but stemness is often a reversible state (plasticity), and degree is contested.
A tumor is a Darwinian clonal population; therapy selects pre-existing resistant subclones; truncal-vs-branch target decides durability.
Immune pressure edits tumors (elimination-equilibrium-escape); a detectable cancer won a hidden contest against surveillance.
Checkpoint blockade releases brakes (CTLA-4 priming, PD-1/PD-L1 killing) on pre-existing primed T cells; CAR-T bypasses MHC.
Primary prevention (tobacco control, viral vaccination) interrupts carcinogenesis at the highest-leverage point; underused for political-economy reasons.
